value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: Japan allows tobacco and alcohol for personal use, but quantity limits apply.
Foreigners can use Visit Japan Web for immigration procedures (immigration clearance, customs declaration) and tax-free purchases during their stay in Japan.
Visitors defined as non-residents will be eligible to use Japan’s new tax refund system starting November 1, 2026.

Foreign visitors and residents in Japan are eligible to receive emergency medical assistance services including medical referrals, emergency patient transports, hospital arrangements, and interpretation services through providers like Emergency Assistance Japan (EAJ).
Short-term stay (less than 90 days) for transit, tourism, business (without paid activities), conference, or study purpose does NOT require a visa for visa exemption countries and regions.
Access to the main temple costs 200 yen
Guided night walk in Kyoto costs $19
City views from Tokyo City Hall panoramic observatory are free
Admission fee to National Museum of Tokyo is 620 yen
Admission ticket to Tokyo Tower starts from $4
Day tickets for international visitors are 8,000 yen ($56.07) per adult and 5,400 yen ($37.85) per child
The average food cost in Japan is $30–50 per day for most travelers in 2026.
Icoca card costs ¥2000 including a ¥500 deposit and ¥1500 credit
Konbini payments make up 10% of purchases in Japan
Average price for a private room in a hostel or guesthouse in Japan is $32 per night
Single-entry tourist visa fee is US$20.00

Duty-free allowance for personal items is up to 200,000 yen total overseas market value; items under 10,000 yen are free of duty and/or tax; no duty-free allowance for items worth more than 200,000 yen each
Visit Japan Web registration and use is completely free of charge
In 2025, the average cost of travel insurance for Japan was $17 per day for a 35-year-old traveler purchasing basic medical travel insurance for a 10-day, $6,000 trip.
Purchases of 1,000,000 JPY (tax-exclusive) or more will be subject to detailed product registration to support tax-free handling and customs verification procedures.
The price for the Japan Rail Pass, an all-you-can-ride ticket for foreign visitors, will increase beginning October 1, 2023, the first price hike in three years.
Starting July 1, 2026, Japan will raise its departure tax from JPY 1,000 (USD 6.47) to JPY 3,000 (USD 19.41) per person for travellers leaving by air or sea.

Suica cards support travel on multiple bullet train lines including Tōhoku Shinkansen (Tokyo to Shin-Aomori), Hokkaidō Shinkansen (Shin-Aomori to Shin-Hakodate-Hokuto), Akita Shinkansen (Tokyo to Akita), Yamagata Shinkansen (Tokyo to Shinjō), Jōetsu Shinkansen (Tokyo to Niigata), and Hokuriku Shinkansen (Tokyo to Kanazawa).

Sparks eSIM provides unlimited data with seamless access to both international and local communication platforms, supported by partnerships with leading Japanese carriers that ensure priority access to high-speed networks and seamless network transitions during high-speed rail travel.
When entering a ryokan, you are expected to remove your footwear according to Japanese customs, leave your footwear in the designated area, and wear slippers provided inside the facility.
Tourists can use a SIM card in Japan to access navigation apps, travel guides, and booking platforms instantly, making travel easier and more efficient.
Ryokan are traditional Japanese-style inns that typically include dinner and breakfast and are recommended to experience traditional Japanese lifestyle.
Hearton Hotel Kyoto is located in Kyoto
Sparks eSIM offers unlimited data with partnerships with leading Japanese carriers, ensuring priority access to high-speed networks and seamless network transitions during high-speed rail travel.
Breakfast/cafes open 7-8am
Sakura Mobile SIM cards can be picked up easily at airports in Japan and are recommended for travelers who prefer shopping locally after arrival.

Amazon.co.jp sells rechargeable WiFi mobile routers with 100GB data valid for 365 days, no contract, no monthly fee, including models like USB Type WiFi Recharge AIR-Stick and Pocket WiFi with gigabit speeds.
Cerulean Tower Tokyu Hotel in Shibuya, Tokyo

Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, requiring the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food, typically prepared by experienced professional chefs.
Japan is still a predominantly cash-based country, with cash payments making up 82% of all payments in 2018.

Small local restaurants in Tokyo can feel intimidating, especially if there’s no English menu or the staff doesn’t speak much English, so being adventurous and stepping outside usual tourist zones helps to eat like a true local.
IC travel cards and smartphone apps can be used to make payments at many convenience stores, shops and vending machines in Japan.
Japan uses many international payment methods such as Apple Pay and Google Pay, and domestic payment methods like PayPay are popular for QR code payments.
Tokyo to Kyoto Shinkansen can be covered in just over two hours
A taxi ride from Haneda Airport to Tokyo takes around 30-40 minutes.
The Japan Rail (JR) Narita Express and K'sei Express deliver riders from the airport to main Tokyo train stations in about 70 minutes.
RailNinja helps travelers compare Shinkansen train types and fares, and provides access to Green Car reservations and specific seat selections to enhance scenic rail journeys.
The 10 most popular IC cards, including Suica, Pasmo, and Icoca, are compatible with each other, allowing travel on almost all train, subway, and bus lines inside Japanese cities.
Residents with bank accounts in Japan can apply for a debit card, but may need to select payment options for online shopping when setting up the card.
A Suica card will be made invalid after 10 years of inactivity.
The Japan Rail Pass is valid for consecutive 7, 14, or 21 days starting from the first day of use.
The validity period of a Japan Rail Pass is the consecutive 7, 14, or 21 days beginning on the date the pass can actually be first used.

The Japan Rail Pass is a multi-day ticket granting unlimited travel on most Japan Railways (JR) services across the country, including shinkansen, limited express, rapid, and local trains, but excluding Nozomi and Mizuho trains unless a supplement fee is paid.
The Japan Rail Pass is valid on almost all trains operated by JR nationwide, including shinkansen, limited express, rapid, and local trains, but generally not valid on non-JR trains.
Suica cards are sold for 1,000, 2,000, 3,000, 4,000, 5,000, or 10,000 JPY, including a 500 JPY partially refundable deposit

You need to purchase a JR Pass Exchange Order from an authorized sales agent outside Japan prior to arrival, then exchange it for the actual JR Pass at Japan Rail Pass exchange offices in Japan within 3 months of issue.
Suica cards can be purchased from ticket machines in most of Tokyo’s main JR rail stations (e.g., Tokyo, Hamamatsucho, Shimbashi, Shinjuku, Shibuya) and JR-EAST Travel Centers.
Physical Suica cards must be purchased in Japan and are commonly available at airports (e.g., Welcome Suica) or train stations upon arrival; they cannot be purchased online before arriving.
Travelers should follow the entry and exit requirements, review local laws, and follow travel guidance from the U.S. embassy or consulate for safe travel in Japan.
Any item whose overseas market value is under 10,000 yen is free of duty and/or tax and is not included in the calculation of the total overseas market value of all articles.
Many convenience stores such as 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson, as well as some chain restaurants and discount stores, operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.
Sapporo is the largest city on the island of Hokkaido.
Noboribetsu is Hokkaido's most famous hot spring resort.
Ginzan Onsen is one of Japan's nicest, old-fashioned onsen towns.
Aizu is a small city known for its samurai history.
Kusatsu Onsen is one of Japan's best hot spring towns.
Yokohama is Japan's second largest city.
Manza Onsen is a hot spring resort with Japan's most sulfuric waters.
Matsumoto is a city famous for its beautiful original castle.
Gujo-Hachiman is a small town in rural Japan with a castle.
Yuzawa is one of Japan's leading ski resort towns.
Nagano is a former Olympic city famous for its temple.
Uji is a city south of Kyoto known for its temple and tea.
Matsuyama is the largest city of Shikoku with a beautiful castle.
Ozu is a small city with a nice castle, old town and villa.
Yufuin is a rural yet trendy hot spring town near Beppu.
Beppu is Japan's capital city of hot springs.
Kumamoto is a modern city most famous for its castle.
Fukuoka is Kyushu’s largest city and an international port connecting Japan to much of Asia.
English is generally understood in major cities and designated tourist sites in Japan.

Visit Japan Web is a service that allows foreigners to perform arrival procedures such as immigration clearance and customs declaration online, as well as use tax-free shopping services during their stay in Japan.

If you want to edit Immigration clearance and Customs declaration you have registered, you can click “Immigration clearance and Customs declaration” on “Japan Entry/Return Procedure Screen” and change it on “Immigration clearance and Customs declaration - Entered Details Confirmation Screen”.

From November 1, 2026, Japan’s tax-free system for inbound travellers will change to a refund-based system where refunds are made according to the individual tax-free shop or contracted refund service after purchase.
Japan has two primary emergency numbers, and knowing both could save your life.

The Japan National Tourism Organization (JNTO) cannot facilitate visa applications nor answer visa-related inquiries; visa application forms can be obtained from the Embassy of Japan in the Philippines website and applications must be submitted through accredited travel agencies, which charge a visa-handling fee that varies per agency.

Many businesses in Japan are cash-only, so travelers should be prepared to carry cash.

Japan does not require proof of travel insurance for entry, but since US health insurance is not accepted there, having a policy with international medical coverage is strongly suggested by the US Department of State.
